findings.message: Target PR #1 changed exactly the approved eight starter files and no extra files.
evidence: GitHub compare main...ag-os/social-media-system-v1-starter lists README.md, package.json, index.html, src/main.js, src/styles.css, src/data/templates.js, src/lib/safety.js, and src/lib/status.js.
Local temp clone file-scope scan matched the approved file manifest.

findings.message: The starter keeps social account connection, credentials, posting, scheduling, analytics API use, and n8n activation blocked by default.
evidence: src/data/templates.js sets mode draft_only, approvalRequired true, livePostingBlocked true, schedulingBlocked true, analyticsApiBlocked true, socialOauthConnected false, credentialsStored false, and n8nAutomationBlocked true.
src/lib/safety.js asserts those draft-only defaults and checks every platform account remains not_connected, draft_only, approvalRequired true, and livePostingBlocked true.
src/main.js renders a Blocked Live Actions Panel.

findings.message: The starter meets the approved Social Media Management System v1 structure, with a useful draft/staging foundation but no browser visual review yet.
evidence: The UI includes Dashboard Overview, Clients, Accounts, Content Intake, Content Calendar, Post Packages, Approval Queue, Weekly Reports, Settings / Config Status, and Blocked Live Actions Panel.
The data model includes multi-client, brand, platform account, content intake, post package, and weekly report templates.
No browser visual regression or accessibility automation was run for this review.
findings.optionalImprovement: Before staging or owner-facing use, run a browser review for responsive layout, accessibility, and copy clarity.

findings.message: Safe local checks passed without dependency installation.
evidence: npm.cmd run check passed in a temp clone of ag-os/social-media-system-v1-starter.
npm.cmd test passed in the same temp clone.
package.json has no dependencies or devDependencies and only node --check based scripts.

findings.message: Code-file scans found no secrets, live calls, forms, tracking scripts, deployment config, paid integrations, hardcoded client name, or fixture markers.
evidence: Precise local scan found no credential/token patterns.
Precise local scan found no fetch, XMLHttpRequest, sendBeacon, form action, tracking, deploy config, or hardcoded client-name patterns.
The starter uses REQUIRED_ placeholders for client, brand, platform, handles, posting volume, approval owner, content pillars, brand voice, and reporting cadence.

optionalImprovements: Before target PR merge, require separate owner approval for the merge action.
Before staging, run browser and responsive review for the static interface.
Before any live social, scheduling, analytics, credential, Netlify, or n8n milestone, require a separate AG OS approval package and approval lock.
evidence: https://github.com/gurnoorbassi/ag-social-media-management-system/pull/1
GitHub compare main...ag-os/social-media-system-v1-starter
Temp clone at C:/Users/Gurnoor/AppData/Local/Temp/ag-os-social-pr-review/ag-social-media-management-system
npm.cmd run check
npm.cmd test
approved file-scope, default-invariant, dependency, package-script, relative-path, and safety scans
limitations: Critique is advisory only and cannot approve, merge, deploy, connect accounts, schedule, post, or activate automations.
Review did not run browser visual regression because the target repo is a static starter with no browser test harness.
Review did not alter target repository files, settings, secrets, branches, or pull request state.
